title: Comprehensive Sentence Creation
learningGoal: The learner will synthesize grammar from the entire curriculum, including potential form, requests, intention, and conditionals, to construct flawless Japanese sentences.
instructions: Translate these four sentences. They test different major grammar points from the past three months. Pay strict attention to your verb conjugations.

contentItems.text: You will need: Potential form, Te-form permissions, Intention form, and Conditional 'tara' form.

writingPrompt: Translate: 1) I can eat sushi. 2) May I take a picture? 3) I intend to go to Japan. 4) If it rains, I will not go.
promptGoal: To construct grammatically accurate sentences across four distinct, advanced paradigms.

wordBank.targetText: とっても いいですか
wordBank.baseTranslation: May I take (Te-form + mo ii desu ka)

referenceHints: Sentence 1: [Sushi] ga [Can eat]. (Potential verbs take 'ga' instead of 'wo').
Sentence 4: [Rain] ga [If it falls], [Will not go].

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: すしが たべられます。しゃしんを とっても いいですか。にほんに いく つもりです。あめが ふったら、いきません。
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Sushi ga taberaremasu. Shashin wo totte mo ii desu ka. Nihon ni iku tsumori desu. Ame ga futtara, ikimasen.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: I can eat sushi. May I take a picture? I intend to go to Japan. If it rains, I will not go.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect application of four distinct grammar structures.

evaluationCriteria.description: Did the learner write 'totte mo ii desu ka'?
Did the learner use plain form verb + 'tsumori desu'?
Did the learner correctly conjugate Furu to 'Futtara'?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: すしが たべられます。しゃしんを とっても いいですか。にほんに いく つもりです。あめが ふったら、いきません。
acceptableAlternates: すしをたべられます。しゃしんをとってもいいですか。にほんへいくつもりです。あめがふったら、いきません。
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Mastery of N5 to N4 grammar points.

feedbackRules.onPass: Brilliant writing! You have demonstrated mastery over the core grammar from the 3-month curriculum.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review your verb conjugations. Check how 'Taberu' becomes 'Taberareru', and how 'Furu' becomes 'Futtara'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Make sure you used the plain form ('iku') before 'tsumori desu', not the masu-form ('ikimasu tsumori').
